claim: Title: How to Read a Paper; Author: S. Keshav; Year: 2007; Venue: SIGCOMM CCR.
evidence: Direct observation of the screenshot's title block via OCR / VLM.
comment: OCR of visible title page. Identification is high confidence. Affiliation text is medium confidence (OCR-dependent).

claim: The paper proposes a three-pass reading method.
section_or_page: Section heading 'The Three-Pass Approach' (visible in screenshot)
evidence: Section heading visible in the screenshot.

claim: Pass 1 = bird's-eye view of the paper.
evidence: Visible text fragment: 'First pass (5–10 minutes): read title, abstract, intro, section headings, conclusion, references; glance at figures and skim the math.'
comment: OCR of a visible fragment. Time budget is part of the visible text, but the rest of the body is not visible.

claim: Pass 1 uses the Five Cs checklist: Category, Context, Correctness, Contributions, Clarity.
evidence: Visible text fragment names the Five Cs explicitly.

claim: Pass 2 takes ~1 hour; Pass 3 takes 1–4 hours and reconstructs the paper.
evidence: Visible fragments: 'Second pass (~1 hour): read the body with care, skipping proofs.' and 'Third pass (1–4 hours): re-implement the paper as if you were its author.'

claim: Full body text, references, figures, and tables are available.
section_or_page: n/a — this claim is FALSE in this run
evidence: NOT visible in the screenshot. This claim is being recorded only to be marked false, so the page is honest about the missing body.
comment: Assertion that the body is missing.
